operating_state: Total revenue increased 27% to $36.5 billion driven by advertising revenue growth to $35.6 billion, with ad impressions up 20% and average price per ad increasing 6% reflecting improved targeting effectiveness despite iOS headwinds. Growth was broadly positive across all regions with particular strength in Asia-Pacific and Rest of World markets, while user engagement remained stable across Facebook and Instagram.
cost_structure: Total costs and expenses increased 6% to $22.6 billion, representing 62% of revenue compared to 75% in the prior year. Research and development expenses rose driven by higher personnel costs and AI infrastructure investments, while cost of revenue increased reflecting higher data center and technical infrastructure operational expenses. Reality Labs investments continue generating substantial operating losses expected to increase meaningfully in 2024.
material_events: The company initiated its first quarterly cash dividend program at $0.50 per share and repurchased $15.0 billion of Class A common stock with $66.4 billion remaining under current authorization. Facilities consolidation restructuring charges were recorded as part of ongoing efficiency measures, while headcount decreased 10% year-over-year. The European Commission opened formal proceedings regarding Digital Markets Act compliance of Meta's consent model.
macro_exposures: Advertising targeting capabilities face continued degradation from Apple's iOS changes and Google's Chrome third-party cookie phase-out beginning January 2024, limiting measurement effectiveness and marketer spending commitments. European regulatory framework including GDPR, DMA, and Digital Services Act creates ongoing compliance costs and operational constraints, with potential service disruptions if EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework is invalidated.
forward_outlook: Management expects Reality Labs operating losses to increase meaningfully in 2024 as metaverse investments accelerate, while maintaining focus on AI infrastructure development and operational efficiency improvements. Capital expenditures are guided to $35-40 billion range focused on servers, data centers, and network infrastructure supporting growing computational demands. The quarterly dividend program and share repurchase activities are expected to continue subject to board discretion.
uncertainty_profile: FTC administrative proceeding seeks substantial modifications to the 2020 consent order including restrictions on minor data usage and product launch limitations, with potential for significant operational constraints. Multiple litigation matters continue including Texas biometric privacy lawsuit seeking material statutory damages and European competition investigations regarding Facebook Marketplace tying allegations. IRS transfer pricing disputes involve potential additional federal tax liability up to $9.0 billion with Tax Court decisions expected in 2024.
